coreConcept: The water cycle is how water moves around Earth in a never-ending loop. The Sun makes water evaporate, it forms clouds, falls as rain or snow, and then gathers again in rivers, lakes, soil, and living things.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Heats water so it turns into vapor and starts the cycle.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Bodies of Water
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Oceans, lakes, and rivers supply most of the water that evaporates into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Atmosphere
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Holds water vapor and moves it around the planet with wind and air currents.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Made of tiny droplets or ice crystals that form when vapor cools and that later produce precipitation.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Land and Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Soil stores water, plants release vapor through transpiration, and runoff or infiltration returns water to bodies of water.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun warms water from oceans, lakes, and soil, turning it into invisible water vapor.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As vapor rises and cools, it turns back into tiny droplets that form clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When droplets grow heavy, they fall as rain, snow, sleet, or hail back to Earth.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection
storyOfProcess.description: Water gathers in rivers, lakes, underground, and in living things, where it can be used again or evaporate once more.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Drives the Cycle
corePrinciples.description: Sunlight provides the heat needed for evaporation and the movement of water through the cycle.

corePrinciples.name: Conservation of Water
corePrinciples.description: Water continually circulates and changes form, but the total amount of water on Earth stays roughly the same.

corePrinciples.name: Phase Changes Matter
corePrinciples.description: Water changes between liquid, vapor, and ice; these changes create clouds, rain, snow, and other weather.
